messages.content: what is one of the tools associated with design thinking? empathy maps behavior driven develpment portfolio canvas set based design

One of the tools associated with design thinking is the empathy map. An empathy map is a visual representation that helps teams understand and empathize with their target users or customers. It is divided into four sections: "Says," "Thinks," "Does," and "Feels." By using an empathy map, designers can gain insights into the user's needs, motivations, and emotions, which can then be used to generate innovative and user-centered solutions.

what is one way to describe a cross fuctional agile team? they release customer products to production cotinuously they are optimised for communication and elivery of value they are made up individuals each whom can define develop test and deploy the system they deliver value every 6 weeks

One way to describe a cross-functional agile team is that they are optimized for communication and the delivery of value. This means that the team consists of individuals who possess a diverse range of skills and expertise necessary to perform tasks at different stages of the product development process. Each team member is empowered to define, develop, test, and deploy the system, ensuring that the entire development lifecycle can be completed within the team. Additionally, these teams aim to deliver customer value continuously, often releasing products to production regularly or in shorter time intervals, such as every 6 weeks.
the primary goal of safe is o achieve what? business agility customer centricity organzing around value lean portfolio management

The primary goal of the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) is to achieve business agility. SAFe provides a set of principles and practices that organizations can follow to implement agile at scale. Business agility refers to an organization's ability to respond and adapt quickly to changing market conditions, customer needs, and competitive pressures. By adopting SAFe, organizations strive to become more flexible, adaptive, and responsive in delivering value to their customers. While SAFe also emphasizes customer centricity, organizing around value, and lean portfolio management, the ultimate aim is to enable business agility.

a confidence vote is taken at the end of pi planning after dependencies are resolved and risks are addressed what best describes the process of the confidence vt the managers vote, business owner vote each person vote the teams and athe arts vote

The best description of the process of a confidence vote in SAFe's Program Increment (PI) planning is that each person, including managers, business owners, team members, and other stakeholders, individually cast their vote indicating their level of confidence in the planned commitments. This means that each participant has an opportunity to express their opinion and vote on their confidence in the PI plan after dependencies are resolved and risks are addressed. The managers, business owners, teams, and even the ARTs (Agile Release Trains) all have a say in the confidence vote, which helps gauge the overall alignment, commitment, and belief in the plan.

which type of decision should remain entralized evene in a decentralized decision making environment decisions that deliver large and broad econimic benefits decisions that come with high cost of delay decisions that are made frequently decisions that require local information

Decisions that deliver large and broad economic benefits should remain centralized even in a decentralized decision-making environment. These types of decisions often have a significant impact on the overall organization or business and require a holistic view and coordination across different departments or teams. Centralizing these decisions ensures that they are made with consideration for the best interests of the entire organization, by taking into account various factors such as financial implications, resource allocation, and strategic alignment. By keeping such decisions centralized, organizations can effectively manage and optimize their resources to maximize the economic benefits derived from those decisions.
which basic agile quality practice reduced bottlenecks ensures consistency definition of done Collective ownership and standards peer review and pairing or established flow

The basic agile quality practice that reduces bottlenecks and ensures consistency is the establishment of flow. Flow refers to the smooth and continuous movement of work through the development process. By optimizing flow, teams can identify and eliminate any bottlenecks or obstacles that may impede progress and slow down the delivery of value. This practice focuses on improving efficiency, minimizing delays, and maintaining a consistent pace of work. It involves streamlining processes, identifying and resolving any issues or dependencies, and ensuring that work moves seamlessly from one stage to another. By establishing flow, teams can achieve a more predictable and efficient delivery of quality outcomes.

the analyzing step of the portfolio kanban system has a new epic with a completed lean businea business case. What best describes the net step for the epic?  :it will be implemented once the epic owner approves the lean business case, it will be moved to the ready state in the portfolio kanban if it receives a go decision from lean portfolio management, it wil remain in the analyzing step until one or mre agile release rains have the cpacity to implement it it will be implemented if it has the highest weighted shortest job first WSJF ranking

The next step for the epic in the analyzing step of the portfolio Kanban system would be to move it to the ready state in the portfolio Kanban if it receives a go decision from lean portfolio management. In the analyzing step, the epic has completed the lean business case, indicating that it has undergone evaluation and analysis to determine its feasibility and value. If it receives approval from the epic owner and meets the criteria set by lean portfolio management, it can then be moved to the ready state in the portfolio Kanban. From there, the epic can be prioritized and scheduled for implementation based on its alignment with strategic goals, available resources, and any other relevant factors considered in lean portfolio management.
ho decides the team PI objective busines value scoring after negotiation: product management the rte the agile team, business owner

The decision regarding the team PI objective business value scoring after negotiation is typically made by the product management in collaboration with the business owner. Product management plays a crucial role in defining the product strategy, prioritizing feature development, and maximizing business value. They work closely with the business owner to understand the desired outcomes and align the team's objectives with the overall business goals. The RTE (Release Train Engineer) and the agile team also provide valuable input during the negotiation process, providing insights into technical feasibility, capacity, and dependencies. However, the ultimate decision-making authority lies with the product management and the business owner, who consider various factors, such as customer feedback, market trends, resource availability, and strategic direction, to determine the team PI objective and the associated business value scoring.

which lean budget guardrail helps ensure the appropiate allocation of budgets to balance near term opportunities with long term strategy and growth : guideing invesments by horizon, continuous business owner engagement, approving significant initiatives, applying capacity allocation

The lean budget guardrail that helps ensure the appropriate allocation of budgets to balance near-term opportunities with long-term strategy and growth is "guiding investments by horizon." This guardrail involves categorizing investments based on different time horizons (e.g., horizon 1 for short-term, horizon 2 for mid-term, and horizon 3 for long-term). By aligning and balancing budgets across these horizons, organizations can ensure that resources are allocated in a way that addresses immediate market needs while also supporting long-term strategic initiatives and growth opportunities. This approach helps prevent a disproportionate focus on short-term gains at the expense of long-term sustainability and innovation.
what else the safe principle unlock the intrinsic otivation of knowledge workers require besides purpose and minimum possible constraints transparency autonomy innovaion incentive based compensation

Besides purpose and minimum possible constraints, the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) principle of unlocking the intrinsic motivation of knowledge workers also requires transparency, autonomy, innovation, and incentive-based compensation.

Transparency is crucial to foster trust and collaboration within teams. By providing visibility into goals, progress, and decision-making processes, transparency helps individuals understand the bigger picture and make informed contributions.

Autonomy allows knowledge workers to have a sense of ownership and control over their work. It enables them to make decisions, experiment, and take responsibility for their outcomes, which can significantly enhance their motivation and creativity.

Innovation is another important aspect, as it encourages continuous learning, improvement, and finding better solutions. Creating an environment that supports experimentation, rewards learning from failures, and promotes a growth mindset can unlock the intrinsic motivation of knowledge workers.

Lastly, incentive-based compensation systems, which tie rewards and recognition to individual and team achievements, can serve as a motivational factor. Such systems can align incentives with desired behaviors, outcomes, and the overall success of the organization, creating an environment that encourages knowledge workers to go above and beyond.

By incorporating these elements, organizations can tap into the intrinsic motivation of knowledge workers, fostering a productive and engaged workforce.
